zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Baker於1562年出生在英國時，她的父親Thomas Baker42歲，母親Agnes40歲。她於1579年6月22日與Henry Rice在英國素福爾可素德波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國米德萊可可拉肯維爾聖 加也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Baker于1562年出生在英国时，她的父亲Thomas Baker42岁，母亲Agnes40岁。她于1579年6月22日与Henry Rice在英国素福爾可素德波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她埋葬在英国米德萊可可拉肯維爾聖 加也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Baker was born in 1562, in England, her father, Thomas Baker, was 42 and her mother, Agnes, was 40. She married Henry Rice on 22 June 1579, in Sudbury, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She was buried in St James' Church, Clerkenwell, Middlesex, England.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Baker nasceu em 1562, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Baker, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Agnes, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Rice em 22 de junho de 1579, em Sudbury, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela foi sepultada em St James' Church, Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Baker родилась в 1562 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Baker было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Rice 22 июня 1579 г. в местности судбури, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она была похоронена в местности ст джемес' чурч, клиркенвелл, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Baker nació en 1562, en Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Baker, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Agnes, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Henry Rice el 22 de junio de 1579, en Sudbury, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Fue sepultada en St James' Church, Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
